common.footer.description: Loopy helps beginners learn programming through small guided steps: observe, edit, run, check, and debug real code.

landing.hero.title: Learn to code in small steps, not by being dropped into an IDE alone.
landing.hero.subtitle: Loopy guides you through a clear flow: observe sample code, edit one line, run the output, check with test cases, then debug small real mistakes.
landing.learn.title: Every lesson has one clear job.
landing.step1.title: Observe
landing.step1.desc: Read a short example, run the sample code, and understand the output before writing on your own.
landing.step2.title: Edit
landing.step2.desc: Change one small part in the editor. Loopy checks the requirement with deterministic rules or tests.
landing.step3.title: Debug
landing.step3.desc: Meet small intentional errors, read the terminal, and debug step by step like real programming.
landing.journey.title: Know where you are and what unlocks next.
landing.journey.desc: Loopy favors a clear journey over a loose catalog: completed, active, and locked lessons are separated so beginners do not have to guess the next step.
landing.pmock.title: Change one line and see the output immediately
landing.pmock.desc: You do not need to read all the theory first. Observe, make a small edit, run it, then check against a clear target.
landing.faq.title: How does Loopy help beginners learn?
landing.faq1.q: How is Loopy different from documentation?
landing.faq1.a: Docs are for reference. Loopy gives you a guided path with the next lesson, an editor, a terminal, automatic checks, and saved progress.
landing.faq2.q: Are Run and Check the same thing?
landing.faq2.a: No. Run only executes your code so you can inspect the output. Check uses rules or test cases to decide whether the lesson requirement is met.
landing.faq3.q: Can I start if I am completely new?
landing.faq3.a: Yes. Early lessons are intentionally small, with examples and concrete requirements so you can move step by step instead of guessing inside an IDE.
landing.bottom.title: Start with one small lesson.
landing.bottom.desc: Choose a learning goal, open the first lesson, and let Loopy guide you through each practice step.

auth.auth.ctx.onboarding: Create an account to save your first path and return to the exact lesson you were learning.
auth.auth.ctx.pvp: Sign in to join challenges, sync results, and return to your match.
auth.auth.ctx.learn: Sign in to save progress, streaks, and the next lesson in your journey.
auth.auth.ctx.onboarding2: Choose your learning goal once, and Loopy will suggest a practical starting point.
auth.auth.ctx.default: Continue your coding journey on Loopy.
auth.auth.title.signup1: Create an account to save your journey.
auth.auth.feat2: Continue from the right next step
auth.auth.feat4: Start free with the core learning path

docs.docs.title: Docs are your reference shelf, not the main path.
docs.docs.subtitle: When you get stuck, use Docs to look up a concept quickly. Then return to the Journey Map to learn in order and save progress correctly.
docs.docs.topic.title: Topics
docs.docs.group1.title: JavaScript basics
docs.docs.group2.title: Python basics
docs.docs.group3.title: When you get stuck
docs.docs.ref.title: console.log helps you inspect output quickly.
docs.docs.ref.desc: For beginners, `console.log` is the simplest way to see what a value is. It works well in Playground and during the Run step inside a lesson.
docs.docs.play.desc: Use it when you want to inspect real output. It executes code; it does not validate the lesson.
docs.docs.check.desc: Return to the lesson so Loopy can grade with rules or test cases. A lesson is complete only after the check passes and progress is saved.
docs.docs.tips.title: How to read output
docs.docs.tips.desc: If the output is not what you expected, change one small variable, run again, and compare. Avoid editing many lines at once while learning.
docs.docs.terminal.desc: Docs explain concepts. Playground lets you try quickly. Learn helps you complete checked lessons.
docs.docs.toc.title: On this page
docs.docs.toc.items: When to use docs|console.log example|Run vs Check|Where to go next
docs.docs.next.title: After reading, try coding.
docs.docs.next.desc: Use Playground to test a concept, or go to Learn for checked practice and saved progress.
docs.docs.bottom.title: Docs support the journey; they do not replace it.
docs.docs.bottom.desc: When you are not sure what to learn next, return to Library instead of wandering through reference pages.

detail.detail.syllabus.title: Learn in small ordered steps, without skipping ahead.
detail.detail.syllabus.desc: Each chapter unlocks through lessons with examples, an editor, output, and clear checks.
detail.detail.empty: No published lessons are available for this path yet.
detail.detail.cta.title: Pick one small lesson to begin.
detail.detail.cta.desc: If you are new, start with the first lesson instead of choosing randomly from too many topics.

languages.languages.title: Choose your first learning path.
languages.languages.subtitle: Each Loopy language path is organized as a small journey with a clear first lesson and real code checks.
languages.languages.browse.title: Available paths
languages.languages.how.title: How does Loopy guide your learning?
languages.languages.cta.title: You do not need to choose perfectly on day one.
languages.languages.cta.desc: Pick the nearest goal. You can change paths after you learn what kind of practice fits you best.

learn.learn.title: One lesson, one thing to do.
learn.learn.subtitle: Loopy splits lessons into small steps so you can understand code, try edits, check correctness, and debug intentional mistakes.
learn.learn.flow.label: 5-step flow
learn.learn.mentor.label: Mentor note
learn.learn.feat1.title: Observe first
learn.learn.feat1.desc: You see sample code and output before being asked to write from scratch.
learn.learn.feat2.title: Run is not grading
learn.learn.feat2.desc: Run only lets you inspect output. It does not mark the lesson as correct.
learn.learn.feat3.title: Check with rules or tests
learn.learn.feat3.desc: When you click Check, the backend validates your answer and saves progress if it is complete.
learn.learn.cta.title: Ready for your first lesson?
learn.learn.cta.desc: Open the Journey Map, choose the active lesson, and work through the editor one step at a time.

library.library.title: Your Journey Map.
library.library.subtitle: Track completed, active, and locked lessons so you always know the next step.
library.library.progress.label: Progress
library.library.next.empty: No suitable next lesson is available yet.
library.library.next.active_desc: This lesson is open. Complete it to unlock the next step in your journey.
library.library.next.locked_desc: This lesson is locked. Finish the previous lesson first.
library.library.next.goal_desc: Completing one small lesson is enough to keep your rhythm.
library.library.lock_rule.desc: Loopy opens lessons in order so beginners do not jump into topics that are too hard too early.
library.library.empty: There are no published lessons in this path yet.
library.library.feat1.title: Done
library.library.feat1.desc: Progress has been saved successfully by the backend.
library.library.feat2.title: Active
library.library.feat2.desc: The lesson you should learn next right now.
library.library.feat3.title: Locked
library.library.feat3.desc: This lesson opens after you finish earlier steps.
library.library.cta.title: Move forward with one small lesson.
library.library.cta.desc: Do not try to learn many topics at once. Choose the active lesson and complete its flow.

onboarding.onboarding.title: Build a learning path that fits you.
onboarding.onboarding.subtitle: Answer a few short questions so Loopy can suggest the easiest starting point to follow.
onboarding.onboarding.goal.title: Why do you want to learn coding?
onboarding.onboarding.goal.desc: Choose the nearest goal. Loopy uses it to suggest your starting path.
onboarding.onboarding.goal.hint: You can change this later, so do not worry about picking perfectly.
onboarding.onboarding.exp.title: Have you coded before?
onboarding.onboarding.exp.desc: Your current experience helps Loopy choose a first lesson that is not too hard or too slow.
onboarding.onboarding.preview.title: Your suggested path
onboarding.onboarding.preview.desc: Start with a small lesson, clear guidance, and real code checks.
onboarding.onboarding.sidebar.desc: Loopy does not force you to choose from dozens of topics on day one.
onboarding.onboarding.sidebar.why.title: Why onboarding matters
onboarding.onboarding.sidebar.why.desc: A clear starting point helps you keep learning and avoid quitting at the first error.

playground.playground.title: Try code quickly without affecting progress.
playground.playground.subtitle: Playground is a sandbox for testing ideas, input, and output. To complete a lesson, return to Learn and click Check.
playground.playground.sandbox_note: This sandbox is saved locally in this browser and does not count as lesson completion.
playground.playground.feat1.title: Try quickly
playground.playground.feat1.desc: Write a few lines of code and inspect the output without opening a lesson.
playground.playground.feat2.title: Separate from grading
playground.playground.feat2.desc: Playground does not grade correctness. It only helps you understand how code runs.
playground.playground.feat3.title: Return to Learn when ready
playground.playground.feat3.desc: To save progress, complete the lesson inside Learn and pass Check.
playground.playground.separate.title: Running code does not always mean it meets the requirement.
playground.playground.separate.desc: Output can look fine, but a lesson needs rules or test cases to confirm the learning goal is met.
playground.playground.cta.title: Want a guided path?
playground.playground.cta.desc: Open the Journey Map to choose the active lesson, get guidance, and save progress after completion.

profile.profile.title: Your learning profile.
profile.profile.subtitle: Review your progress, streak, points, and the next lesson in your Loopy journey.
profile.profile.journey.title: You are building a coding habit one step at a time.
profile.profile.next.title: Recommended next lesson
profile.profile.next.headline: Return to the step that is already open.
profile.profile.next.desc: Continue the active lesson instead of picking a random new topic.
profile.profile.today.title: One small lesson is enough.
profile.profile.today.desc: Today’s goal: complete one lesson with a successful Check.
profile.profile.notice.title: Progress only increases after a successful save.
profile.profile.notice.desc: Loopy does not celebrate before the backend confirms the lesson is complete.
profile.profile.journey.tab.title: Your saved journey
profile.profile.journey.tab.desc: This summary uses saved progress and account data. Open Journey Map for the exact next lesson.
profile.profile.journey.tab.card2: The library page separates completed, current, next, and locked lessons so you do not have to guess.
profile.profile.goals.tab.title: Learning goals
profile.profile.goals.tab.desc: Keep the goal small enough to act on today. You can update your path without losing saved progress.
profile.profile.notifications.tab.title: In-app notifications
profile.profile.notifications.tab.desc: These reminders are shown inside Loopy. Email and push delivery are not enabled until backend support exists.
profile.profile.notifications.item1: Show a reminder when the next lesson is available.
profile.profile.notifications.item2: Show a note when progress was saved successfully.
profile.profile.notifications.item3: Show guidance before entering PvP too early.
profile.profile.settings.tab.title: Account settings
profile.profile.settings.tab.desc: Only safe account summary actions are shown here. Destructive actions need backend support first.

pvp.pvp.title: Code in real-time challenges with other learners.
pvp.pvp.subtitle: Join a 1v1 room, solve a small question, and compare how you handled it. PvP helps you practice reactions; it does not replace the main learning path.
pvp.pvp.locked.title: Complete a few lessons before entering PvP.
pvp.pvp.locked.desc: PvP is easier after you are comfortable with Run, Check, and reading output inside Learn.
pvp.pvp.mode.title: Choose a mode
pvp.pvp.mode.1v1.desc: Match with one learner in a short round focused on a single question.
pvp.pvp.mode.br.title: Battle Royale
pvp.pvp.mode.br.desc: The multi-player mode is being prepared. Start with 1v1 first.
pvp.pvp.diff.title: Difficulty
pvp.pvp.feat1.title: Small questions
pvp.pvp.feat1.desc: Focus on one specific skill instead of a problem that is too large.
pvp.pvp.feat2.title: Realtime feedback
pvp.pvp.feat2.desc: Track match state, time, and submissions inside the same room.
pvp.pvp.feat3.title: Practice after learning
pvp.pvp.feat3.desc: PvP works best for reviewing skills you have already practiced in the Journey Map.
pvp.pvp.unlock.desc: Complete lessons first, then use PvP to improve speed and confidence.
pvp.pvp.header.title: PvP Coding Room
pvp.pvp.match.notfound.title: Match not found
pvp.pvp.match.notfound.desc: The room code may be wrong or the match may have ended. Check the code or create a new match.
